original: The journal Science is adding an extra round of statistical checks to its peer-review process, editor-in-chief Marcia McNutt announced today.
translation_literal: 《科学》杂志正在给它的同行评审流程增加一轮额外的统计检查，主编玛西娅·麦克纳特今天宣布。

chunks.english: an extra round of statistical checks
chunks.relation: 作 is adding 的宾语

chunks.chinese: 到它的同行评审流程中
chunks.relation: 说明增加到哪里

chunks.chinese: 主编玛西娅·麦克纳特今天宣布
chunks.relation: 说明消息来源

sentence_patterns.explanation: editor-in-chief Marcia McNutt announced today 放在句末，说明前面内容是谁宣布的。

original: The policy follows similar efforts from other journals, after widespread concern that basic mistakes in data analysis are contributing to the irreproducibility of many published research findings.
translation_literal: 这项政策是在广泛担忧之后跟随了其他期刊的类似努力，即数据分析中的基本错误正在导致许多已发表研究发现的不可重复性。

chunks.relation: 作 follows 的宾语
说明政策出现的背景

chunks.english: that basic mistakes in data analysis are contributing to the irreproducibility of many published research findings
chunks.chinese: 即数据分析中的基本错误正在导致许多已发表研究发现的不可重复性
chunks.relation: 解释 concern 的具体内容

chunks.chinese: 数据分析中的基本错误
chunks.relation: 同位语从句内部主语
修饰 basic mistakes
同位语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: the irreproducibility of many published research findings
chunks.chinese: 许多已发表研究发现的不可重复性
chunks.relation: 作 are contributing to 的宾语

chunks.chinese: 许多已发表研究发现的
chunks.relation: 修饰 irreproducibility

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 抽象名词 + that 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: that basic mistakes... 解释 widespread concern 的具体内容。

original: “Readers must have confidence in the conclusions published in our journal,” writes McNutt in an editorial.
translation_literal: 麦克纳特在一篇社论中写道：“读者必须对我们期刊发表的结论有信心。”

chunks.english: confidence in the conclusions published in our journal
chunks.chinese: 对我们期刊发表的结论的信心
chunks.relation: 作 must have 的宾语

chunks.chinese: 麦克纳特在一篇社论中写道
chunks.relation: 说明引语来源
说明宾语短语的核心词是 confidence
过去分词短语作后置定语，修饰 conclusions

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 直接引语 + writes + 主语
sentence_patterns.explanation: writes McNutt 位于引语后，是引语来源倒装。

original: Working with the American Statistical Association, the journal has appointed seven experts to a statistics board of reviewing editors (SBoRE).
translation_literal: 通过与美国统计协会合作，该期刊已经任命了七位专家进入一个评审编辑统计委员会。

chunks.english: Working with the American Statistical Association
chunks.chinese: 通过与美国统计协会合作

chunks.relation: 作 has appointed 的宾语

chunks.english: to a statistics board of reviewing editors (SBoRE)
chunks.chinese: 进入一个评审编辑统计委员会
chunks.relation: 补充说明专家被任命到哪里

original: Manuscripts will be flagged up for additional scrutiny by the journal’s internal editors, or by its existing Board of Reviewing Editors or by outside peer reviewers.
translation_literal: 稿件将被该期刊的内部编辑、其现有评审编辑委员会或外部同行评审者标记出来以接受额外审查。

chunks.relation: 说明标记目的

chunks.english: by the journal’s internal editors, or by its existing Board of Reviewing Editors or by outside peer reviewers
chunks.chinese: 由该期刊的内部编辑、现有评审编辑委员会或外部同行评审者
chunks.relation: 说明动作执行者
被动语态中 by 短语列出的第一类执行者

chunks.english: its existing Board of Reviewing Editors
chunks.relation: 被动语态中 by 短语列出的第二类执行者
被动语态中 by 短语列出的第三类执行者

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: by A, or by B or by C
sentence_patterns.explanation: 三个 by 短语并列说明可能标记稿件的主体。

original: The SBoRE panel will then find external statisticians to review these manuscripts.
translation_literal: SBoRE 小组随后将寻找外部统计学家来评审这些稿件。

chunks.relation: 作 find 的宾语
说明寻找统计学家的目的

original: Asked whether any particular papers had impelled the change, McNutt said: “The creation of the statistics board was motivated by concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research and is part of Science’s overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish.”
translation_literal: 当被问及是否有任何特定论文推动了这一改变时，麦克纳特说：“统计委员会的建立是由对科学研究中统计和数据分析应用的广泛担忧所推动的，并且是《科学》提高我们发表的研究可重复性的整体努力的一部分。”

chunks.english: Asked whether any particular papers had impelled the change
chunks.chinese: 当被问及是否有任何特定论文推动了这一改变时
chunks.relation: 相当于 When she was asked...，说明 McNutt 说话前的提问背景

chunks.english: The creation of the statistics board was motivated by concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research and is part of Science’s overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish
chunks.chinese: 统计委员会的建立是由对科学研究中统计和数据分析应用的广泛担忧所推动的，并且是《科学》提高我们发表的研究可重复性的整体努力的一部分
chunks.relation: 作 said 的引语内容

chunks.english: was motivated ... and is part of ...
chunks.chinese: 受到推动并且是……的一部分
chunks.relation: 说明 The creation of the statistics board 的两个并列谓语结构
修饰 research
whether 宾语从句内部主语
whether 宾语从句内部谓语
作 had impelled 的宾语

chunks.english: The creation of the statistics board
chunks.relation: said 后引语宾语从句内部主语

chunks.english: whether any particular papers had impelled the change
chunks.chinese: 是否有任何特定论文已经推动了这一改变
chunks.relation: 作 Asked 的宾语内容
引语宾语从句中第一个谓语

chunks.english: concerns broadly with the application of statistics and data analysis in scientific research
chunks.chinese: 对科学研究中统计和数据分析应用的广泛担忧
chunks.relation: 作介词 by 的宾语，说明推动原因
与 was motivated by 并列，说明同一主语的另一性质

chunks.english: part of Science’s overall drive to increase reproducibility in the research we publish
chunks.chinese: 《科学》提高我们发表的研究可重复性的整体努力的一部分
chunks.relation: 作 is 的表语

chunks.english: to increase reproducibility in the research we publish
chunks.chinese: 提高我们发表的研究的可重复性
chunks.relation: 不定式作后置定语，说明 overall drive 的目的内容

sentence_patterns.explanation: Asked... 省略了主语和 be 动词，可理解为 When McNutt was asked...。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: was motivated... and is part of...
sentence_patterns.explanation: 引语内部两个谓语并列，共同说明 statistics board 的建立。

original: Giovanni Parmigiani, a biostatistician at the Harvard School of Public Health, a member of the SBoRE group, says he expects the board to “play primarily an advisory role”.
translation_literal: 哈佛公共卫生学院的生物统计学家、SBoRE 小组成员乔瓦尼·帕米贾尼说，他预计该委员会将“主要发挥咨询作用”。

chunks.english: a biostatistician at the Harvard School of Public Health
chunks.chinese: 哈佛公共卫生学院的生物统计学家
chunks.relation: 解释主语身份

chunks.english: a member of the SBoRE group
chunks.relation: 继续解释主语身份

chunks.english: he expects the board to play primarily an advisory role
chunks.chinese: 他预计该委员会将主要发挥咨询作用
chunks.relation: 作 says 的宾语
宾语从句「he expects the board to play primarily an advisory role」内部主语
宾语从句「he expects the board to play primarily an advisory role」内部谓语
宾语从句「he expects the board to play primarily an advisory role」内部宾语，作用于「he expects the board to play primarily an advisory role」

chunks.english: to play primarily an advisory role
chunks.relation: 补充说明 board 将做什么

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 专有名词 + 多个同位语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个同位语连续解释 Giovanni Parmigiani 的身份，主干是 Giovanni Parmigiani says...。

original: He agreed to join because he “found the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact.”
translation_literal: 他同意加入，因为他“发现 SBoRE 建立背后的远见是新颖的、独特的，并且可能产生持久影响。”

chunks.english: because he found the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact
chunks.chinese: 因为他发现 SBoRE 建立背后的远见是新颖的、独特的，并且可能产生持久影响
chunks.relation: 说明同意加入的原因
原因状语从句内部主语
原因状语从句内部谓语

chunks.english: the foresight behind the establishment of the SBoRE
chunks.relation: 作 found 的宾语

chunks.english: behind the establishment of the SBoRE
chunks.chinese: 在 SBoRE 建立背后的
chunks.relation: 修饰 foresight

chunks.english: to be novel, unique and likely to have a lasting impact
chunks.chinese: 新颖、独特并可能产生持久影响
chunks.relation: 补充说明 found 的宾语 foresight 的性质
宾语补足语中的第一个并列表语
宾语补足语中的第二个并列表语

chunks.english: likely to have a lasting impact
chunks.relation: 宾语补足语中的第三个并列表语

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: adj., adj. and likely to do
sentence_patterns.explanation: novel, unique and likely to have... 并列作宾语补足语。

original: This impact will not only be through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places that may want to model their approach after Science.
translation_literal: 这种影响将不仅通过《科学》本身的发表物实现，而且有望通过更多可能想要仿效《科学》方法的出版机构实现。

chunks.english: not only through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places
chunks.chinese: 不仅通过《科学》本身的发表物，而且有望通过更多出版机构
chunks.relation: 作 will be 的表语

chunks.english: that may want to model their approach after Science
chunks.chinese: 可能想要仿效《科学》方法的
chunks.relation: 修饰 publishing places

chunks.english: through the publications in Science itself
chunks.chinese: 通过《科学》本身的发表物
chunks.relation: 表语「not only through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places」中的并列部分

chunks.english: through a larger group of publishing places
chunks.relation: 表语「not only through the publications in Science itself, but hopefully through a larger group of publishing places」中的并列部分
定语从句「that may want to model their approach after Science」内部主语
定语从句「that may want to model their approach after Science」内部谓语
定语从句「that may want to model their approach after Science」内部宾语，作用于「that may want to model their approach after Science」

sentence_patterns.explanation: not only through... but through... 构成并列，说明影响扩散的两个渠道。

original: John Ioannidis, a physician who studies research methodology, says that the policy is “a most welcome step forward” and “long overdue”.
translation_literal: 研究研究方法论的医生约翰·伊奥尼迪斯说，这项政策是“非常受欢迎的一步进展”，并且“早该如此”。

chunks.english: a physician who studies research methodology
chunks.chinese: 一位研究研究方法论的医生
chunks.relation: 解释主语身份
修饰 physician

chunks.english: that the policy is a most welcome step forward and long overdue
chunks.chinese: 这项政策是非常受欢迎的一步进展并且早该如此
chunks.relation: 作 says 的宾语
定语从句「who studies research methodology」内部主语
定语从句「who studies research methodology」内部谓语
定语从句「who studies research methodology」内部宾语，作用于「who studies research methodology」
宾语从句「that the policy is a most welcome step forward and long overdue」内部主语
宾语从句「that the policy is a most welcome step forward and long overdue」内部谓语

chunks.english: a most welcome step forward and long overdue
chunks.chinese: 非常受欢迎的一步进展并且早该如此
chunks.relation: 宾语从句「that the policy is a most welcome step forward and long overdue」内部表语，作用于「that the policy is a most welcome step forward and long overdue」

sentence_patterns.explanation: a physician... 是 John Ioannidis 的同位语身份说明。

original: “Most journals are weak in statistical review, and this damages the quality of what they publish.”
translation_literal: “大多数期刊在统计审查方面薄弱，而这损害了它们所发表内容的质量。”

chunks.english: Most journals are weak in statistical review
chunks.chinese: 大多数期刊在统计审查方面薄弱

chunks.english: and this damages the quality of what they publish
chunks.chinese: 而这损害了它们所发表内容的质量
chunks.relation: 与前一分句并列
作 of 的宾语
第一并列分句的主语
第一并列分句的谓语
第一并列分句的表语
第二并列分句的主语，指代前一分句所说情况
第二并列分句的谓语

chunks.english: the quality of what they publish
chunks.chinese: 它们所发表内容的质量
chunks.relation: 第二并列分句的宾语
what 宾语从句内部主语，指 journals
what 宾语从句内部谓语

sentence_patterns.explanation: 前一分句提出问题，后一分句说明后果。

original: “I think that, for the majority of scientific papers nowadays, statistical review is more essential than expert review,” he says.
translation_literal: 他说：“我认为，对于如今大多数科学论文来说，统计审查比专家审查更重要。”

chunks.english: that statistical review is more essential than expert review
chunks.chinese: 统计审查比专家审查更重要
chunks.relation: 作 think 的宾语

chunks.english: for the majority of scientific papers nowadays
chunks.chinese: 对于如今大多数科学论文来说
chunks.relation: 限定判断范围
说明引语来源
宾语从句内部主语
宾语从句内部谓语
宾语从句内部表语

sentence_patterns.explanation: for the majority... 插在 that 从句中，阅读时可先跳过。

original: But he noted that biomedical journals such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet pay strong attention to statistical review.
translation_literal: 但是他指出，像《内科学年鉴》、《美国医学会杂志》和《柳叶刀》这样的生物医学期刊非常重视统计审查。

chunks.english: that biomedical journals such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet pay strong attention to statistical review
chunks.chinese: 像《内科学年鉴》、《美国医学会杂志》和《柳叶刀》这样的生物医学期刊非常重视统计审查
chunks.relation: 作 noted 的宾语

chunks.english: such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet
chunks.chinese: 例如《内科学年鉴》、《美国医学会杂志》和《柳叶刀》
chunks.relation: 举例说明 biomedical journals

chunks.english: biomedical journals such as Annals of Internal Medicine, the Journal of the American Medical Association and The Lancet
chunks.chinese: 诸如《内科学年鉴》《美国医学会杂志》和《柳叶刀》等生物医学期刊
chunks.relation: 宾语从句内部主语
宾语从句内部谓语

chunks.chinese: 对统计审查的高度关注
chunks.relation: 作 pay 的宾语

sentence_patterns.explanation: such as 后的三个期刊名很长，主干是 biomedical journals pay strong attention...。

original: Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data, but statistical errors are alarmingly common in published research, according to David Vaux, a cell biologist.
translation_literal: 据细胞生物学家戴维·沃克斯说，专业科学家被期望知道如何分析数据，但统计错误在已发表研究中普遍得令人担忧。

chunks.english: Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data
chunks.chinese: 专业科学家被期望知道如何分析数据

chunks.english: but statistical errors are alarmingly common in published research
chunks.chinese: 但统计错误在已发表研究中普遍得令人担忧
chunks.relation: 与前一分句转折

chunks.english: according to David Vaux, a cell biologist
chunks.chinese: 据细胞生物学家戴维·沃克斯说
chunks.relation: 说明观点来源
解释 David Vaux 身份
并列分句「Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data」中的主语部分
并列分句「Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data」中的谓语部分
并列分句「Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data」中的宾语部分，作用于「Professional scientists are expected to know how to analyse data」
插入语「according to David Vaux, a cell biologist」中的主语部分
插入语「according to David Vaux, a cell biologist」中的谓语部分
插入语「according to David Vaux, a cell biologist」中的宾语部分，作用于「according to David Vaux, a cell biologist」

chunks.chinese: 具备统计素养的评审者
chunks.relation: 同位语「a cell biologist」中的并列部分

chunks.english: editors who can verify the process
chunks.relation: 同位语「a cell biologist」中的并列部分

sentence_patterns.explanation: according to David Vaux... 位于句末，说明整句判断来源。
a cell biologist 是 David Vaux 的同位语。

original: Researchers should improve their standards, he wrote in 2012, but journals should also take a tougher line, “engaging reviewers who are statistically literate and editors who can verify the process”.
translation_literal: 他在2012年写道，研究人员应该提高他们的标准，但期刊也应该采取更严格的立场，“聘用具备统计素养的评审者和能够核查过程的编辑”。

chunks.chinese: 研究人员应该提高他们的标准

chunks.relation: 说明观点来源

chunks.english: but journals should also take a tougher line
chunks.chinese: 但期刊也应该采取更严格的立场
chunks.relation: 与前一分句转折

chunks.english: engaging reviewers who are statistically literate and editors who can verify the process
chunks.chinese: 聘用具备统计素养的评审者和能够核查过程的编辑
chunks.relation: 说明采取严格立场的具体方式
修饰 reviewers
修饰 editors

sentence_patterns.explanation: he wrote in 2012 插在两个分句之间，阅读时可先跳过。
reviewers 和 editors 并列，各自带一个定语从句。

original: Vaux says that Science’s idea to pass some papers to statisticians “has some merit, but a weakness is that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify the papers that need scrutiny in the first place”.
translation_literal: 沃克斯说，《科学》把一些论文交给统计学家的想法“有一些优点，但一个弱点是它依赖评审编辑委员会首先识别需要审查的论文”。

chunks.english: that Science’s idea to pass some papers to statisticians has some merit, but a weakness is that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify the papers that need scrutiny in the first place
chunks.chinese: 《科学》把一些论文交给统计学家的想法有一些优点，但一个弱点是它依赖评审编辑委员会首先识别需要审查的论文
chunks.relation: 作 says 的宾语

chunks.english: to pass some papers to statisticians
chunks.chinese: 把一些论文交给统计学家的
chunks.relation: 修饰 idea

chunks.english: that it relies on the board of reviewing editors to identify the papers that need scrutiny in the first place
chunks.chinese: 它依赖评审编辑委员会首先识别需要审查的论文
chunks.relation: 作 a weakness is 的表语
修饰 papers

chunks.english: Science’s idea to pass some papers to statisticians
chunks.chinese: 《科学》把一些论文交给统计学家的想法
chunks.relation: 宾语从句第一分句的主语
宾语从句第一分句的谓语
作 has 的宾语
宾语从句第二分句的主语
宾语从句第二分句的谓语
表语从句内部主语，指这一想法
表语从句内部谓语
作 relies on 的宾语

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: idea 与谓语被不定式定语隔开
sentence_patterns.explanation: Science’s idea to pass... 是主语，后面的 has some merit 才是谓语。

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: a weakness is + that 从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: that it relies... 说明 weakness 的具体内容。
